name: Binary Representation
description: Binary represents numbers using only 0 and 1. Repeated division by 2 or bit shifts can reveal each bit.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to reverse collected remainders
Not handling n = 0

name: Bitwise AND
description: The AND operator keeps a bit set only when both input bits are 1.
commonMistakes: Using logical && instead of bitwise &
Forgetting operator precedence in combined expressions

name: Bitwise OR
description: The OR operator sets a bit if either input bit is 1.
commonMistakes: Using OR where XOR was intended
Not building the mask first

name: Bitwise XOR
description: XOR sets a bit when the input bits differ. It is useful for toggling bits and finding unique values.
commonMistakes: Using XOR without understanding cancellation
Mixing XOR and OR in the same trick

name: Bitwise NOT
description: The NOT operator flips every bit in a number.
commonMistakes: Forgetting signed integer representation
Expecting only positive outputs

name: Left Shift
description: 1 << i creates a mask with only the ith bit set.
commonMistakes: Using Math.pow instead of shift
Confusing bit position from 0-based and 1-based indexing

name: Right Shift
description: Right shift moves bits to the right and can expose a target bit at the least significant position.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong shift count
Forgetting to mask with 1

name: Odd Even Check
description: A number is odd when its least significant bit is 1, so n & 1 is enough.
commonMistakes: Using modulo even when the exercise expects bitwise practice
Comparing against true instead of 1

name: Check ith Bit
description: Build a mask or shift the number so the target bit can be tested.
commonMistakes: Using 1-based indexing accidentally
Shifting the mask in the wrong direction

name: Set ith Bit
description: Use OR with a mask to force a bit to 1.
commonMistakes: Using XOR instead of OR
Forgetting to save the updated number

name: Clear ith Bit
description: Use AND with the negated mask to force a bit to 0.
commonMistakes: Negating the number instead of the mask
Missing parentheses around the shift

name: Toggle ith Bit
description: Use XOR with a mask to flip the target bit.
commonMistakes: Using OR so the bit never flips back
Building the wrong mask

name: Count Set Bits
description: Count how many 1 bits appear in the binary form of a number.
commonMistakes: Looping forever on negative numbers
Using string conversion for an intended bit solution

name: Brian Kernighan Algorithm
description: Repeatedly remove the lowest set bit using n & (n - 1).
commonMistakes: Using it without understanding why one bit disappears
Forgetting to increment the counter

name: Power of Two
description: A positive power of two has exactly one set bit, so n > 0 and (n & (n - 1)) == 0.
commonMistakes: Not guarding against n <= 0
Using loops when constant time is expected

name: XOR Tricks
description: XOR cancels equal values and keeps unmatched values, which helps in parity and unique-number problems.
exampleJava: int ans = 0; for (int num : nums) ans ^= num;
commonMistakes: Not initializing accumulator to 0
Trying XOR on problems where counts are not paired

name: Single Number
description: When every other number appears twice, XORing all values leaves the unique number.
exampleJava: int ans = 0; for (int num : nums) ans ^= num;
commonMistakes: Sorting first and missing the intended O(1) extra space trick
Using hash maps for a bitwise practice question

name: Two Unique Numbers
description: Use total XOR to find a distinguishing bit, then partition numbers into two groups.
commonMistakes: Choosing the wrong distinguishing mask
Forgetting to split into two XOR groups

name: Missing Number
description: The XOR of indices and values leaves the missing number in a 0..n array.
commonMistakes: Using sum formula when the exercise wants XOR practice
Forgetting to XOR with n at the end

name: Subsets Using Bits
description: Every number from 0 to 2^n - 1 can act as a selection mask for subset generation.
commonMistakes: Using the wrong upper bound for masks
Reading the wrong bit while building a subset

name: Bitmasking Basics
description: A bitmask compresses multiple yes/no states inside one integer.
commonMistakes: Mutating the wrong state variable
Forgetting to clear a bit during backtracking
